src/main/java/dev/arnaldo/seizure/manage.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/dev/arnaldo/seizure/manage.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial, the compiler may warn or error depending on configuration, and it is required for correct serialization metadata in modern Java.

src/main/java/dev/arnaldo/seizure/manage.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the @Serial annotation.
src/main/java/dev/arnaldo/seizure/manage.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is defined in java.io.Serial, which must be imported for the code to compile.

src/main/java/dev/arnaldo/seizure/manage.general_idea: Add @Serial annotation to serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/dev/arnaldo/seizure/manage.why_exists: Java 21 enforces stricter serialization checks; without @Serial, the code may fail to compile with --enable-preview or strict linting rules introduced in newer JDKs.

src/main/java/dev/arnaldo/seizure/manage.general_idea: Import java.io.Serial to support the new annotation.
src/main/java/dev/arnaldo/seizure/manage.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is not available without importing java.io.Serial, causing a compile error.

src/main/java/dev/arnaldo/seizure/manage.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 14+ serialization rules.
src/main/java/dev/arnaldo/seizure/manage.why_exists: Starting with Java 14, the @Serial annotation is required on serialization-related members (like serialVersionUID) to avoid compiler warnings or errors when strict serialization checks are enabled, ensuring compatibility with modern JDKs.
